evals.name: clear-multi-step-new-project
evals.prompt: Set up an outcome for this task: **Plan holiday to Japan** — Want to go for two weeks in October, need to sort flights, accommodation, itinerary, and travel insurance. Container type: sub-project.
evals.expected_output: Should propose an outcome-oriented name, suggest an area, propose a concrete first action, and infer active status from the October timeframe. Should not create a full plan with multiple tasks.

assertions.name: proposes-outcome-name
assertions.criteria: The response proposes an outcome name that describes a finished state or result — NOT an activity. It should NOT start with a verb like 'Plan', 'Sort', 'Set up', 'Organise'. Good examples: 'Japan holiday booked', 'Two-week Japan trip planned and booked'. Bad examples: 'Plan holiday to Japan', 'Plan Japan trip'.

assertions.name: suggests-area
assertions.criteria: The response suggests or asks about which area (top-level parent project) the outcome should live under.

assertions.name: identifies-first-action
assertions.criteria: The response proposes or asks about a concrete first action — a single step the user can take to get started.

assertions.name: does-not-create-full-plan
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT create multiple sub-tasks, sections, or a detailed plan. At most one first action.

evals.name: merge-with-existing-outcome
evals.prompt: Set up an outcome for this task: **Sort out home insurance renewal** — Current policy expires next month, need to compare quotes and switch provider. I already have a project called 'Home Admin' for household paperwork. Container type: sub-project.
evals.expected_output: Should detect the existing 'Home Admin' project and propose merging rather than creating a new outcome. Should ask one question at a time — merge first, then first action later.

assertions.name: proposes-merge
assertions.criteria: The response acknowledges the existing 'Home Admin' project and suggests adding this work there rather than creating a brand new outcome.

assertions.name: asks-one-question
assertions.criteria: The response asks exactly one question — the merge question. It does not also ask about first action, area, or priority in the same response.

assertions.name: does-not-create-new-outcome
assertions.criteria: The response does not create a separate new project or parent task — it proposes using the existing one.

evals.name: someday-outcome
evals.prompt: Set up an outcome for this task: **Learn woodworking** — Always fancied making furniture. Maybe take a course or start with some YouTube tutorials. No rush. Container type: sub-project.
evals.expected_output: Should recognise this as someday from the 'no rush' and 'maybe' language. Should apply the someday label and keep the outcome in its natural area — NOT move it to a separate Someday/Maybe area.

assertions.name: identifies-someday
assertions.criteria: The response identifies this as a someday item, not an active priority. It picks up on cues like 'no rush', 'maybe', 'always fancied'.

assertions.name: applies-someday-label
assertions.criteria: The response applies (or proposes applying) a someday label to the outcome. It does NOT move the outcome to a separate Someday/Maybe area or project.

assertions.name: stays-in-natural-area
assertions.criteria: The outcome stays in (or is proposed for) its natural area based on content — NOT moved to a separate someday area. For woodworking, this would be a Personal or Hobbies area.

assertions.name: still-creates-first-action
assertions.criteria: The response still creates or proposes a first action, even for a someday item.

evals.name: outcome-needs-refining
evals.prompt: Set up an outcome for this task: **Sort out finances** — Things are a mess, bills everywhere, no budget, credit card debt building up. Container type: sub-project.
evals.expected_output: Should ask what outcome the user is after since 'sort out finances' is too vague. Should not assume what the outcome is about.

assertions.name: asks-about-outcome
assertions.criteria: The response asks the user what specific outcome they're after — it doesn't just accept 'Sort out finances' as the outcome name.

assertions.name: asks-one-question
assertions.criteria: The response asks exactly one question, not multiple questions at once.

assertions.name: does-not-assume-scope
assertions.criteria: The response does not assume what the outcome should cover or create an outcome without clarifying first.

evals.name: urgent-active-project
evals.prompt: Set up an outcome for this task: **Move house** — We've exchanged contracts, completion is on April 15th. Need to sort removals, redirect mail, update address everywhere, and cancel utilities. Container type: sub-project.
evals.expected_output: Should infer this is clearly active (concrete date, exchanged contracts). Should not ask about someday. Should propose outcome-oriented name and first action.

assertions.name: infers-active
assertions.criteria: The response treats this as an active outcome without asking about active vs someday. The April 15th date and exchanged contracts make this obviously active.

assertions.name: sets-deadline-on-first-action
assertions.criteria: The response sets or proposes setting a deadline on the first action, since April 15th is a hard external date. The deadline should be picked up from context without asking separately.

assertions.name: proposes-outcome-name
assertions.criteria: The response proposes an outcome name that describes a finished state — NOT an activity. It should NOT start with a verb like 'Move', 'Sort', 'Set up'. Good examples: 'Moved into new house', 'House move completed by April 15th'. Bad examples: 'Move house', 'Complete house move'.

assertions.name: identifies-first-action
assertions.criteria: The response proposes a concrete first action.

assertions.name: does-not-create-full-plan
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT create multiple sub-tasks or a detailed plan. At most one first action.

evals.name: project-with-obvious-area
evals.prompt: Set up an outcome for this task: **Prepare Q2 board presentation** — Need to compile financials, write the narrative, get design to make the deck, and rehearse. Container type: sub-project.
evals.expected_output: Should clearly assign to a Work area without asking. Should propose outcome-oriented name and first action.

assertions.name: assigns-work-area
assertions.criteria: The response assigns or proposes the outcome under a Work area (or equivalent business/professional area) without needing to ask.

assertions.name: proposes-outcome-name
assertions.criteria: The response proposes an outcome name that describes a finished state — NOT an activity. It should NOT start with a verb like 'Prepare', 'Create', 'Make'. Good examples: 'Q2 board presentation ready', 'Q2 board deck delivered'. Bad examples: 'Prepare Q2 board presentation', 'Create board deck'.

assertions.name: identifies-first-action
assertions.criteria: The response proposes a concrete first action — a single step to get started.

assertions.name: does-not-ask-unnecessary-questions
assertions.criteria: The response does not ask questions that have obvious answers from context (e.g. doesn't ask about area when it's clearly work, doesn't ask about active vs someday for a Q2 deadline).

evals.name: outcome-not-activity-name
evals.prompt: Set up an outcome for this task: **Organise garage** — It's full of junk, can barely get the car in. Need to clear stuff out, put up shelving, and get some storage boxes. Container type: sub-project.
evals.expected_output: Should propose an outcome-based name describing the finished state (e.g. 'Garage cleared and organised') rather than an activity-based name. Should suggest area and first action.

assertions.name: name-describes-finished-state
assertions.criteria: The proposed outcome name describes a finished state or result — what 'done' looks like. Good examples: 'Garage cleared and organised', 'Garage usable again'. Bad examples: 'Organise garage', 'Clear out garage', 'Sort out garage'.

assertions.name: name-does-not-start-with-verb
assertions.criteria: The proposed outcome name does NOT start with an activity verb like 'Organise', 'Clear', 'Sort', 'Set up', 'Fix', 'Tidy', 'Clean'. Outcome names should not be phrased as actions.

assertions.name: suggests-area
assertions.criteria: The response suggests or asks about which area (top-level parent project) the outcome should live under.

assertions.name: identifies-first-action
assertions.criteria: The response proposes or asks about a concrete first action — a single step the user can take to get started.

evals.name: project-with-hard-external-deadline
evals.prompt: Set up an outcome for this task: **Apply for the small business grant** — State government grant for new businesses, up to $10k. Applications close April 30. Need to write a business plan, get two references, and fill in the online form. Container type: sub-project.
evals.expected_output: Should propose an outcome-oriented name, identify this as active (hard deadline), set a deadline of April 30 on the first action, and propose a concrete first step. Should not ask about active vs someday.

assertions.name: proposes-outcome-name
assertions.criteria: The response proposes an outcome name that describes a finished state — NOT an activity. It should NOT start with a verb like 'Apply'. Good examples: 'Small business grant application submitted', 'State government grant secured'. Bad examples: 'Apply for grant', 'Small business grant application'.

assertions.name: sets-deadline-april-30
assertions.criteria: The response sets or proposes setting a deadline of April 30 on the first action, since applications close on that date. This should be picked up from context without asking separately about deadlines.

assertions.name: infers-active
assertions.criteria: The response treats this as an active outcome without asking about active vs someday. The April 30 application closing date makes it clearly active.

assertions.name: identifies-first-action
assertions.criteria: The response proposes a concrete first action — a single step to get started.

evals.name: parent-task-lightweight
evals.prompt: Set up an outcome for this task: **Get car serviced** — Due for its annual service. Need to book the mechanic, drop it off, and pick it up. Container type: parent task.
evals.expected_output: Should create a parent task (not a sub-project) with an outcome name, first sub-task, suggest an area, and infer active status. Should be quick — this is a simple outcome.

assertions.name: proposes-outcome-name
assertions.criteria: The response proposes an outcome name that describes a finished state — NOT an activity. Good examples: 'Car serviced and roadworthy', 'Annual car service completed'. Bad examples: 'Get car serviced', 'Service the car'.

assertions.name: creates-parent-task-not-project
assertions.criteria: The response creates (or proposes creating) a parent task with a sub-task for the first action — NOT a sub-project. It should be clear the container is a task, not a project.

assertions.name: first-action-is-sub-task
assertions.criteria: The first action is created as a sub-task of the parent task, not as a standalone task in a project.

assertions.name: suggests-area
assertions.criteria: The response suggests or asks about which area the parent task should live under.

assertions.name: does-not-over-engineer
assertions.criteria: The response does not create multiple sub-tasks, sections, or treat this as a complex project. Just the parent task and one first sub-task.

evals.name: strips-stale-metadata-on-conversion
evals.prompt: Set up an outcome for this task: **Fix the leaking shower** — The shower head drips constantly. Need to replace the washer and maybe the cartridge. Currently has priority p2, due date of next Monday, and labels 'next' and 'home'. Container type: parent task.
evals.expected_output: Should strip the scheduling metadata (priority, due date, next/context labels) silently during conversion since they don't belong on an outcome container. Should still propose an outcome name, area, and first action as normal.

assertions.name: strips-priority
assertions.criteria: The response strips or resets the priority (from p2 to p4/default) as part of converting the task to a parent task. This should happen silently without asking the user.

assertions.name: strips-due-date
assertions.criteria: The response removes the due date as part of the conversion. Due dates on parent tasks create noise in the Today view.

assertions.name: strips-next-and-context-labels
assertions.criteria: The response removes the 'next' and 'home' labels as part of the conversion. These are action-level labels that don't belong on an outcome container.

assertions.name: does-not-ask-about-stripping
assertions.criteria: The metadata stripping happens silently as part of the conversion — the response does NOT ask the user whether to remove the priority, due date, or labels.

assertions.name: proposes-outcome-name
assertions.criteria: The response still proposes an outcome name describing a finished state, despite the metadata cleanup.

evals.name: parent-task-someday
evals.prompt: Set up an outcome for this task: **Fix the squeaky gate** — The front gate squeaks every time you open it. Probably just needs some WD-40 and tightening the hinges. Maybe a new latch too. No rush, it's been like that for months. Container type: parent task.
evals.expected_output: Should create a parent task with someday label applied, kept in its natural area (not moved to a separate Someday/Maybe area). Should still propose a first sub-task.

assertions.name: identifies-someday
assertions.criteria: The response identifies this as a someday item based on 'no rush' and 'been like that for months'.

assertions.name: applies-someday-label
assertions.criteria: The response applies (or proposes applying) a someday label to the parent task. It does NOT move it to a separate Someday/Maybe area or project.

assertions.name: creates-parent-task-not-project
assertions.criteria: The response creates (or proposes creating) a parent task — NOT a sub-project.

assertions.name: stays-in-natural-area
assertions.criteria: The outcome stays in its natural area (e.g. Personal or Home) — NOT moved to a separate someday area.

assertions.name: still-creates-first-action
assertions.criteria: The response still creates or proposes a first sub-task, even for a someday item.
